title: Coverage, Thresholds, and Meaningful Evidence · Independent Template Trial
summary: Set coverage policy that exposes risk without encouraging empty tests. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a changed-code coverage and exception policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret line, branch, function, condition, and path coverage limitations.
Choose global, package, and changed-code thresholds from risk and baseline.
Exclude generated or unreachable code with reviewed narrow rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-coverage-review-6xlvsn: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Coverage Review into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-coverage-review-6xlvsn:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

stimulus.title: Coverage Review failure evidence

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Coverage Review condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Coverage Review applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Pair coverage gaps with mutation, defect, and risk evidence.
